Create Individual Isochrone Maps and Shapefiles

Source: R/create_individual_isochrone_plots.R
mysterycall_plot_isochrones.Rd

This function creates individual Leaflet maps and shapefiles for specified drive times based on isochrone data.

Usage

mysterycall_plot_isochrones(isochrones, drive_times, output_dir = NULL)

Arguments

isochrones

An sf object containing isochrone data.

drive_times

A vector of unique drive times (in minutes) for which maps and shapefiles will be created.

output_dir

Directory where HTML maps and shapefiles are saved. Defaults to a session-specific folder inside tempdir().

Value

Called for its side effect of writing per-drive-time map HTML files and shapefiles to disk. Returns NULL invisibly.

See also

Other mapping: mysterycall_clear_isochrone_cache(), mysterycall_create_isochrones(), mysterycall_isochrones_for_df(), mysterycall_map_acceptance_rate(), mysterycall_map_acog_districts(), mysterycall_map_base(), mysterycall_map_block_group(), mysterycall_map_leaflet(), mysterycall_map_physicians(), mysterycall_plot_density(), mysterycall_plot_line(), mysterycall_plot_scatter()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # interactive()
# Load required libraries
library(sf)
library(leaflet)
library(mysterycall)

# Load isochrone data
isochrones <- readRDS("path_to_isochrones.rds")

# List of unique drive times for which you want to create plots and shapefiles
drive_times <- unique(isochrones$drive_time)

# Create individual isochrone maps and shapefiles
mysterycall_plot_isochrones(isochrones, drive_times)
}
